title: Kurmanathaswamy Temple, Srikurmam
tagline: Kurmanathaswamy Temple, Srikurmam — spiritual town of Andhra Pradesh

overview.short: The Kurmanathaswamy temple, also known as the Kurmanatha temple, Srikurma or Srikurmam temple, is a Hindu temple dedicated to Kurma – the second avatar of Vishnu.
overview.description: The Kurmanathaswamy temple, also known as the Kurmanatha temple, Srikurma or Srikurmam temple, is a Hindu temple dedicated to Kurma – the second avatar of Vishnu. It is located in Srikurmam village, Srikakulam district in Andhra Pradesh, India. According to Prapannamrutam and earliest inscription of the temple, In saka 1035 CE Anantavarman Chodaganga Deva of Eastern Ganga Dynasty of Odisha converted this temple to a Vaishnava khetra from a Siva temple.The temple was Built before 11th-century CE in a fusion architecture of kalinga Architecture and Dravidian Architecture. topPlaces.name: Sri Kurmam
topPlaces.description: Sri Kurmam also known as Srikurmu or Srikurma is a village near Srikakulam, Andhra Pradesh, India, 14.5 km southeast of Srikakulam town in the Gara mandal of Srikakulam district. It was named after the Srikurmam temple dedicated to Kurma avatar of the Hindu god Vishnu, which was re-established by Eastern Ganga Dynasty king Anantavarman Chodaganga Deva.

topPlaces.name: Salihundam
topPlaces.description: Salihundam, is a village and panchayat in Gara Mandal of Srikakulam district in Andhra Pradesh. It is a historically important Buddhist monument of ancient Kalinga and a major tourist attraction It is a village lying on top of the hill on the south bank of the Vamsadhara River.

topPlaces.name: Kallepalli
topPlaces.description: Pedda Ganagalla Peta or Ganagalla Peta is a village located around 5 kilometers from Srikakulam town in Andhra Pradesh, India. River Nagavali near the village Ganagalla Peta.

topPlaces.name: Nagavali River
topPlaces.description: The River Nagavali also known as Langulya is one of the main rivers of Southern Odisha and Andhra States in India, between Rushikulya and Godavari basins.

It was named after the Srikurmam temple dedicated to Kurma avatar of the Hindu god Vishnu, which was re-established by Eastern Ganga Dynasty king Anantavarman Chodaganga Deva.

Salihundam, is a village and panchayat in Gara Mandal of Srikakulam district in Andhra Pradesh. It is a historically important Buddhist monument of ancient Kalinga and a major tourist attraction It is a village lying on top of the hill on the south bank of the Vamsadhara River.
Pedda Ganagalla Peta or Ganagalla Peta is a village located around 5 kilometers from Srikakulam town in Andhra Pradesh, India. River Nagavali near the village Ganagalla Peta.
The River Nagavali also known as Langulya is one of the main rivers of Southern Odisha and Andhra States in India, between Rushikulya and Godavari basins.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Kurmanathaswamy Temple, Srikurmam?
faq.a: The best time to visit Kurmanathaswamy Temple, Srikurmam is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Kurmanathaswamy Temple, Srikurmam?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Visakhapatnam Airport (103 km) and the nearest railway station is Visakhapatnam Junction (96 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Kurmanathaswamy Temple, Srikurmam?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Kurmanathaswamy Temple, Srikurmam at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Kurmanathaswamy Temple, Srikurmam?
faq.a: Stays in Kurmanathaswamy Temple, Srikurmam start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Kurmanathaswamy Temple, Srikurmam?
faq.a: The top attractions are Sri Kurmam, Salihundam, Kallepalli, Nagavali River.
